summary: High‑energy, jam‑heavy show with standout Let It Grow and a smoking Scarlet > Fire transition; recording sounds solid though source is unknown
review: The performance bursts with energy from the first set onward, highlighted by an epic Let It Grow, a killer Scarlet/Fire transition jam, soaring solos in Saint of Circumstance and Stella, plus a twisted Spanish Jam and a hot Sugar Magnolia. Jerry’s playing is described as especially into it, even jamming with the drummers after Saint of Circumstance. No recording flaws are mentioned, suggesting a clean, listenable capture.

ai_rating.rationale: Single five‑star review praises the musical performance heavily; recording quality is not discussed but presumed good, yielding an overall high rating with low confidence due to limited data.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific audio complaints; reviewers imply the sound is clear enough to enjoy the performance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts normally then launches into sustained high‑energy jams; the second half maintains momentum with extended improvisations and a strong, fiery close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as fully into the music, delivering powerful guitar work and even jamming with the drummers after Saint of Circumstance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Gets a featured jam spot with Jerry post‑Saint of Circumstance, indicating solid rhythmic support.
